Compile failures are wire diagnostics: a dependency cycle among
modules surfaces with the library's message at the declaration.
-- go.mod --
module app

go 1.26

require github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/assetmapper v0.0.0

replace github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/assetmapper => ASSETSDIR
-- main.go --
package main

import "os"

func main() { os.Exit(run()) }
-- web/assets.go --
package web

import "embed"

//fabrik:assets
//go:embed all:assets
var Assets embed.FS
-- web/assets/a.js --
import "./b.js";
-- web/assets/b.js --
import "./a.js";
-- want/diags --
error: web/assets.go:5:1: assetmapper: dependency cycle among assets: a.js, b.js
  help: assets are compiled and checked at generation time; fix the tree and rerun
